summary: I read `guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md`, then checked the dark-mode change under review (CSS token layer, `ThemeHelper`, `StatusHelper`, the Stimulus `theme_controller`, the init-script/toggle partials, both layouts, sidebar, and the two Minitest files) against them — verifying the service/Result and thin-controller rules (N/A: no business surface added; the one helper is correctly a helper), the Tailwind semantic-token mechanism and shade mapping, the reserved status-color soft-badge + dark pattern kept in a single source of truth, the class-based `@custom-variant dark`, a11y (real button, `aria-label`/`aria-pressed`, status word always shown), and Minitest coverage, plus confirming via grep that the token sweep left no raw neutral utilities in app chrome and that `ui-style-guide.md` was updated in the same PR per the CLAUDE.md mandate. I re-examined the two minor points prior iterations flagged and found neither is an actual guide violation under the guide as amended (the flash neutral branch is the guide-sanctioned gray soft-badge status form with the prescribed `dark:` variants, and the toggle's `outline` focus indicator satisfies the "visible indigo focus indicator" a11y requirement and matches a pattern already on `main`), so I wrote a **pass** verdict with empty findings to `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/guide-alignment-critic/verdict.json`.
